preamble: The palatalized voiced alveolar plosive [dʲ] can undergo a complex phonetic transformation to become the voiceless retroflex affricate [ʈʂ]. This shift involves several significant changes: the sound loses its voicing (devoicing), transforms from a simple stop into an affricate (a stop followed by a fricative release), and its place of articulation moves from the alveolar ridge to a retroflex position, where the tongue tip curls backward. The initial palatalization is also lost in favor of retroflexion.
phoneticEffects: Devoicing, Affrication, Retroflexion, Loss of Palatalization

examples.from: Middle Chinese 知組 (zhi group) dʲ
examples.note: This represents a historical sound change where a palatalized alveolar stop evolved into a voiceless retroflex affricate.
